resources.string:
• Heat pumps are by far the most efficient heating technology.

• They convert 1 kWh of electricity into 3-4 kWh of heat on average.

• Ground-source heat pumps are more efficient than air-source heat pumps but also have a higher installation cost.

• By the way: Fridges and freezers use the same thermodynamic process as a heat pump, moving heat from inside the appliance to the outside.

• Sources: Prof. Dipl. –Ing. W. Schenk, Hochschule München, 2020 and Greenhouse Media GmbH, 2022 (accessed on 14.04.2025).

• In the EU, the main use of energy by households is for residential heating, followed by water heating.

• In 2022, the residential sector accounted for 26% of the total final energy consumption in the EU.

• Source: Eurostat, 2024 (accessed on 14.04.2025).

• Replacing fossil-fuel heating with a heat pump can significantly reduce CO₂ emissions.

• When powered by renewable electricity, heat pumps can operate with almost no greenhouse gas emissions

• An increased share of renewable energy in the grid results in lower greenhouse gas emissions.

• Source: Bundesverband Wärmepumpe (BWP) e.V., 2024 (accessed on 14.04.2025).

• Heat pumps can enhance power grid stability by providing flexibility in balancing supply and demand, especially when combined with smart grid technology.

• Heat pumps can shift their energy consumption by storing heat during periods of high renewable energy production and using it when demand is higher.

• By taking simple, voluntary measures to shift their energy consumption, heat pumps can effectively relieve the load on the electricity grid and noticeably reduce the need for grid expansion.

• Source: Fraunhofer IEE - Flexible Wärmepumpen im Verteilnetz, 2022 (accessed on 14.04.2025).
Congratulations! You made it to the High Score list!
1. Consume electricity to heat up the building or the hot water tank.
Switch between heating the building or the hot water tank.
2. Only use electricity from solar and wind to save CO₂.
3. Do not use electricity generated from gas or coal.
4. Use solar and wind power when the solar and wind indicators are below the red line in the center of the screen.
5. To improve your comfort score, air the room or take a shower by tapping the floating buttons.\n This will lower the temperature in the building and the hot water in the tank.
